name: Lebanon
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday): **THE LEBANON FRONT BECAME THE PROXIMATE CAUSE OF TWO STATE-ON-STATE MISSILE EXCHANGES IN 72H — ISRAEL STRUCK BEIRUT'S DAHIYEH SUBURB JUNE 7 (≥2 KILLED, 11-20 WOUNDED; FIRST CAPITAL STRIKE SINCE THE RENEWED TRUCE, CROSSING THE BEIRUT RED-LINE), TRIGGERING IRAN'S FIRST BALLISTIC SALVO SINCE APRIL 8 AND ISRAELI STRIKES ON IRAN JUNE 8 BEFORE BOTH STOOD DOWN. THEN TYRE: A FIRST-EVER WHOLE-CITY EVACUATION ORDER (JUNE 8) AND THE DEADLIEST TYRE RAID OF THE WAR (JUNE 9, ≥8 KILLED), CONTINUING AFTER THE ISRAEL-IRAN SUSPENSION; CHRISTIAN LEADERS APPEALED INTERNATIONALLY TO SPARE THE CHRISTIAN DISTRICT.** (CONFIRMED ACTION — ISW, Al Jazeera, NPR, Axios) After Hezbollah fired rockets near Yiftach, Israel struck a 'Hezbollah command centre' in Beirut's southern Dahiyeh suburb — the first capital strike since the renewed truce — crossing the exact Beirut red-line the June 7 brief flagged as holding; Iran answered ~22:00 June 7 with its first ballistic salvo since the April 8 ceasefire, Israel struck deep into Iran June 8, and both sides stood down within 24h under Trump pressure. Israel's freedom of action then narrowed to Lebanon, where the escalation went: a first-ever evacuation order for the ENTIRE city of Tyre (June 8) and the deadliest Tyre raid of the war June 9 (≥8 killed), continuing AFTER the Israel-Iran mutual suspension; Christian leaders appealed internationally to spare the Christian district. Ceasefire wreckage: the Israel-Hezbollah truce (~June 1-3; Israel not to strike Beirut suburbs — broken June 7; Hezbollah not to attack Israel — broken by the Yiftach rockets) is dead; Hezbollah formally rejected the Washington framework June 4 demanding full Israeli withdrawal. Cumulative Lebanese toll ~3,500 dead, >1.2M displaced. GlobalSecurity names the Lebanon front as the PRIMARY escalation driver of the wider war — twice in 72h the proximate cause of state-on-state missile exchanges. Erdogan (June 10) declared Israel's strikes on Lebanon and Syria a threat to Turkey that 'must be stopped' — the first Turkish escalatory entry this cycle (RHETORIC, watch item). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: the 'US greenlights OR Israel launches an expanded operation against Hezbollah' trigger (ACTIVE) STAYS ACTIVE and INTENSIFIED — the Dahiyeh strike, whole-city Tyre evacuation and deadliest Tyre raid are intensification-within-active. The Beirut red-line (the watched 'attack on Beirut -> full-scale resumption' threshold) was CROSSED June 7 by a CONFIRMED Israeli strike on Dahiyeh — document as confirmed action; it produced the first Iranian salvo since April but BOTH sides stood down within 24h, so it did NOT become full-scale war resumption. The 'Israeli civilian casualty' trigger STAYS WATCHING — the dead are in Lebanon/Iran, not Israeli civilians. No new trigger to active beyond the existing Lebanon-operation one. Day 100 / 2026-06-07 (Sunday): **LEBANON ESCALATED — THREE ISRAELI SOLDIERS HAVE NOW BEEN KILLED IN THE SOUTH SINCE THE WEEK-OLD TRUCE, AND SATURDAY'S ISRAELI STRIKES KILLED NINE INCLUDING THREE LEBANESE *ARMY* PERSONNEL — BUT BEIRUT WAS AGAIN NOT STRUCK (IRAN'S RED-LINE INTACT).** (intel A2, CONFIRMED ACTION) Hezbollah, having flatly rejected the 3-June Washington-brokered truce, kept up fire on Israeli forces over the weekend, killing three Israeli soldiers since the ceasefire was signed (Capt. Lemberg Thu, Sgt. Yaari Fri, Capt. Galma Sat). Israel continued striking the south: Saturday 6 June airstrikes killed nine, including three Lebanese ARMY members (a strike on Saksakiyah killed six civilians). Defence Minister Katz reiterated the army 'will continue operations in southern Lebanon,' demanded a demilitarised zone, and said Israel 'would not be withdrawing.' Two second-order signals: (i) Israel killing Lebanese ARMY personnel undercuts the Beirut government it signed the truce with; (ii) Israeli MILITARY casualties (three in four days) raise domestic pressure and make the 'no ceasefire in Lebanon' posture costlier. The single most important detail is unchanged and geographic: the strikes hit the SOUTH (Tyre/Sidon/Saksakiyah/Nabatieh), NOT Beirut — the specific trigger Araghchi has tied to 'full-scale resumption.' **TRIGGER (June 7): the 'US greenlights OR Israel launches an expanded military operation against Hezbollah' trigger (ACTIVE, window by 2026-06-08) is INTENSIFYING-WITHIN-ACTIVE — Israeli soldier deaths + Katz's no-withdrawal + Lebanese-army casualties are an intensifying expanded operation, though still sub-Beirut. NOT a new activation; Beirut red-line NOT crossed, so no Iran-resumption trigger moves.** Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5-6 June). **THE 3-JUNE CEASEFIRE IS FRAYING — ≥21 KILLED IN SOUTHERN LEBANON FRIDAY, EVACUATION ORDERS NORTH OF THE LITANI, AOUN CALLS IT A 'LAST CHANCE' AND PUBLICLY CRITICISES IRAN — BUT BEIRUT WAS NOT STRUCK.** Out of the 4th round, Israel and Lebanon agreed (3 June) to renew the ceasefire with 'pilot/safe zones,' but the fighting never stopped: Israeli strikes killed AT LEAST 21 in southern Lebanon on Friday 5 June (CONFIRMED — Al Jazeera, CBS) — seven in Tyre overnight, plus a Sidon municipal council member, two Syrian children and a paramedic in Zebdine. Israel ordered residents of three villages north of the Litani to EVACUATE ahead of expected operations. President Joseph Aoun framed the renewed deal as Lebanon's 'last chance' for a comprehensive truce AND publicly CRITICISED Iran amid the Israeli attacks (Al Jazeera liveblog, 5 June) — a notable crack in the Beirut-Tehran line. CRUCIALLY the strikes hit the SOUTH (Tyre/Sidon/Nabatieh), NOT Beirut — the specific trigger Iran (Araghchi) tied to a 'full-scale resumption' — so Iran's Beirut red-line is INTACT / NOT crossed. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 6)**: the expanded-operation trigger STAYS ACTIVE (the south-Lebanon war continues; a fraying-but-renewed ceasefire is not a resolution). NO trigger to active — Beirut was NOT struck, so any Iran-resumption / full-scale-war trigger STAYS WATCHING. The 'Israeli civilian casualty' trigger STAYS WATCHING — the dead are in Lebanon, not Israeli civilians. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **A WASHINGTON-BROKERED CEASEFIRE WAS ANNOUNCED — THEN HEZBOLLAH REJECTED IT, FIGHTING CONTINUED, AND IT KILLED THE FIRST ISRAELI SOLDIER SINCE THE ANNOUNCEMENT AND A UNIFIL PEACEKEEPER. THE SINGLE BIGGEST MOVER OF THE WINDOW.** Out of the 4th round of Israel-Lebanon talks at the US State Department, a ceasefire was announced to take effect early Thursday 4 June — and it immediately fractured: **Hezbollah publicly REJECTED it**, with a Hezbollah official telling NPR it will not accept ANY ceasefire that does not BEGIN with Israeli withdrawal from south Lebanon (CONFIRMED — NPR, Boston Globe). Lebanese President Joseph Aoun called the deal the 'last chance' for a comprehensive truce and said it would enter force within 24 hours of ALL parties approving — a tacit acknowledgement it had not yet been accepted. Fighting did not stop: an **Israeli soldier, Capt. Eitan Shmuel Lemberg (21), was killed by a Hezbollah anti-tank missile** in southern Lebanon Thursday afternoon — the first Israeli soldier killed since the announced ceasefire (CONFIRMED — IDF via Times of Israel); **Israeli strikes killed at least 4 in Lebanon** (CONFIRMED — Boston Globe); and a **Serbian UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed** (Salvadoran + Spanish peacekeepers wounded) by mortars near Marjayoun late Wednesday (CONFIRMED — UNIFIL via NPR). Crucially, **Trump publicly claimed Hezbollah did NOT reject the offer and that 'progress is being made'** (ToI liveblog) — a direct, on-the-record contradiction of Hezbollah's own statement and of the body count (a one-side-framing artifact, not a fact). Iran tied **any attack on Beirut to a 'full-scale resumption of the war'** (Araghchi). The status is now neither holding nor collapsed: announced, rejected, and being violated. Lebanon remains the explicit precondition Iran uses to justify the US-talks stall — a FAILED ceasefire keeps the channel jammed; a holding one is the off-ramp; right now it is neither. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 4)**: the expanded-operation trigger STAYS ACTIVE (the south-Lebanon war continues; an announced-but-rejected-and-violated ceasefire is not a resolution). The 'Israeli civilian casualty' trigger STAYS WATCHING — the new deaths are a SOLDIER (Capt. Lemberg) and a UNIFIL PEACEKEEPER, NOT an Israeli civilian; no trigger moves to active (the casualties are not US/allied-military; Hezbollah still calibrates off Israeli-civilian targets). PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **THE 4TH ROUND OF ISRAEL-LEBANON TALKS CONVENED IN WASHINGTON WHILE ISRAEL KEPT BOMBING THE SOUTH (≥8 KILLED) — AND TRUMP'S LEASH ON NETANYAHU WENT PUBLIC.** The 4th round of Israel-Lebanon negotiations opened at the US State Department on 2 June (two-day, described by Lebanese sources as 'preparatory' ahead of substantive 3 June talks on phased Israeli withdrawal, Lebanese-army deployment in the south, and ceasefire-enforcement / 'move-versus-move' mechanics). The 1 June Beirut cap is HOLDING — Israel did NOT strike Beirut's southern suburbs and Hezbollah has not resumed cross-border rocket fire — but Israeli bombardment continued to pound southern Lebanon, killing at least 8 (Lebanon National News Agency) 'hours after Trump announced de-escalation.' Israel also eased civil-defence restrictions in northern Israel (a de-escalation marker). The headline tonal development: Trump confirmed he told Netanyahu in their 1 June call 'You're f---ing crazy. You'd be in prison if not for me' (Axios), angry that the Lebanon escalation was imperilling the Iran talks; Netanyahu reframed it as 'tactical disagreements.' This is negotiate-while-bombing with a US-imposed partial cap and now-public ally friction — directly tied to Iran's suspension, since Lebanon is the explicit precondition Iran is using to justify it. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 3)**: the expanded-operation trigger STAYS ACTIVE (south-Lebanon war continues; the Beirut cap is a partial de-escalation, not a resolution). The 'Israeli civilian casualty' trigger STAYS WATCHING. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **TRUMP BROKERED A PARTIAL, CONTESTED DE-ESCALATION — ISRAEL HELD OFF THE BEIRUT-SUBURBS STRIKE BUT KEPT HITTING SOUTH LEBANON; A NEW IDF GOLANI KIA; CROSS-BORDER ROCKET INTENSITY DROPPED. On 1 June Trump asked Netanyahu NOT to strike Beirut's southern suburbs and announced Israel and Hezbollah had agreed to 'halt hostilities'; the Lebanese presidency confirmed receipt of 'Hezbollah's agreement to the US proposal... a reciprocal cessation of attacks' (CONFIRMED — Al Jazeera, The Tribune, Lebanese presidency). BUT it is CONTESTED and PARTIAL: Israel did NOT independently confirm a ceasefire, Netanyahu's office said south-Lebanon operations 'would continue' if Hezbollah keeps targeting Israeli civilians, and Axios reported a 'tense phone conversation' between Trump and Netanyahu. On 2 June Israel kept striking south Lebanon while holding off the Beirut attack (CONFIRMED — US News/Reuters, Al-Monitor, UN News). NEW ESCALATION MARKER: an IDF Golani Brigade soldier was killed and five wounded in a clash with Hezbollah in south Lebanon (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel) — a fresh in-window IDF combat death. DE-ESCALATION on the cross-border axis: Hezbollah did NOT announce new cross-border rocket attacks on 2 June (vs 50+ on 31 May), only two operations against IDF forces INSIDE south Lebanon; Israel intercepted two projectiles overnight; schools reopened in northern Israel. Lebanese MoPH toll 3,433 killed / 10,395 injured (+21 vs 31 May). The 4th round of Israel-Lebanon political talks convened in DC 2-3 June (State track). **CASUALTY-COUNT FLAG:** a 2 June report cites the IDF death toll rising 'to 12' with the Golani KIA — this CONTRADICTS the 24 IDF combat deaths reconciled on 30 May; treat the Golani KIA as CONFIRMED (a fresh combat death) but do NOT elevate the conflicting '12' cumulative — flagged for reconciliation. Net: the Beirut red line was walked back under Trump pressure (de-escalation), but the south-Lebanon war and an IDF death continued — negotiate-while-bombing, now with a partial cap. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 2)**: the expanded-operation trigger STAYS ACTIVE (south-Lebanon war continues; Beirut capped is a partial de-escalation, not a resolution). The new Golani KIA is a CONFIRMED second in-window IDF combat death (the first being the mid-May Litani-crossing KIA) — feeds the israel-node 'second IDF KIA in the 45-day window' sub-trigger (see israel node). The 'Israeli civilian casualty' trigger STAYS WATCHING (the KIA is a SOLDIER, not a civilian). PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **LEBANON CROSSED WASHINGTON'S RED LINE — IDF CONFIRMED OPERATING INSIDE BEIRUT AND ACROSS THE LITANI, EVEN AS THE FIRST PENTAGON ISRAEL-LEBANON MILITARY TALKS OPENED THE SAME DAY. On 29 May Netanyahu announced IDF forces had crossed the Litani and were 'operating in Beirut and the Bekaa Valley' — the first IDF action in Beirut in ~3 weeks — calling it a 'tactical victory' (CONFIRMED — Netanyahu on record; Times of Israel; Wikipedia Timeline of the 2026 Lebanon war). The 28 May brief had flagged that WASHINGTON WARNED ISRAEL AGAINST STRIKING THE CAPITAL to protect the Iran talks — so Beirut operations are a NEW red-line crossing. Israel issued sweeping evacuation orders for ~7 southern towns/villages ahead of strikes south of the Zahrani River; hundreds of thousands displaced. The Lebanese health ministry reported 142 killed in the prior 72 hours, raising the cumulative toll to ~3,355 (from ~3,244 at the 28 May brief). IDF combat deaths reached 24 — RECONCILED UPWARD from the 28 May brief's '22' baseline (a Givati Brigade female soldier killed + 2 reservists wounded by a Hezbollah drone on 27 May). Crucially, all of this ran SIMULTANEOUSLY with the first-ever direct Israel-Lebanon military talks at the Pentagon on 29 May (see A4) — the negotiate-while-bombing pattern, here as a SINGLE coercive strategy, not two data points. The expanded-operation trigger STAYS ACTIVE (fired May 27); the Beirut crossing intensifies it. Michael Rubin (defense analyst): any ceasefire is 'illusionary' while Hezbollah keeps heavy weapons and is excluded from the arrangement — the structural problem behind the Pentagon track.** Day 90 of Hormuz blockade (Thursday May 28). **THE GROUND WAR GRINDS ON — FIRST CONFIRMED GROUND-ENGAGEMENT IDF KIA, RISING LEBANESE TOLL, PENTAGON COORDINATION MEETING TOMORROW (MAY 29). Israeli forces continued expanding ground operations beyond the Yellow Line and north of the Litani on May 27, fighting Hezbollah around Zawtar Sharqieh and Yohmor al-Shaqif (IDF had not established control of Zawtar Sharqieh by afternoon); the IDF struck near the Qaraoun Dam (Litani River Authority: no direct damage). One IDF soldier was killed and another seriously wounded by a Hezbollah drone May 27 — the first ground-engagement IDF KIA, raising the IDF military-death baseline to 22 since March 2. Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health reported 3,244 cumulative deaths and 9,777 wounded as of May 28 — +28 deaths over 24h including children, the highest single-day wounded increment since the May 15 ceasefire extension. NO Beirut strike reported (Washington warned Israel against striking in Beirut to protect the Iran talks; targeted assassinations there remain notionally permitted). NEAR-TERM: a Lebanese-Israeli-American military coordination meeting at the Pentagon on May 29 (tomorrow), ahead of the fourth round of direct Israel-Lebanon peace talks June 2-3 in Washington. Lebanon remains the deal's binding constraint (Iran insists any deal end 'the war on all fronts including Lebanon'). The expanded-operation trigger STAYS ACTIVE (fired May 27).** PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 of Hormuz blockade (Wednesday May 27). **ISRAEL CROSSED THE LITANI — THE EXPANDED OPERATION IS LAUNCHED; 31+ KILLED INCL. CHILDREN; LEBANON TRIGGER FIRES (CONFIRMED).** Israel expanded its ground operation beyond the security zone and across the Litani River, striking 100+ Hezbollah sites overnight; Lebanon's health ministry reported at least 31 killed including children (Mashghara ~12; a village north of the Litani ~12), with evacuation warnings for Nabatiyeh province (CONFIRMED — Euronews May 27 carrying the Lebanon Health Ministry figures; Algemeiner May 26; Times of Israel liveblogs). Netanyahu: 'The IDF are operating with large forces on the ground and seizing strategic areas.' Washington APPROVED the expansion (warning Israel only against striking in Beirut to protect the Iran talks). The May-25 trigger (US greenlights OR Israel launches an expanded operation against Hezbollah within 14 days, by 2026-06-08) is satisfied on BOTH limbs by confirmed action → moved to active. The expanded op jeopardises the separate US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire track (next talks June 2-3) and directly collides with Iran's deal precondition that any agreement 'end the war on all fronts including Lebanon' — the unsynchronised Lebanon track is the deal's key vulnerability. PRIOR (May 25): Day 87 of Hormuz blockade (Monday May 25). **LEBANON FRONT HOT — THE 'CEASEFIRE HOLDS / HEZBOLLAH NON-RETALIATION HOLDS' FRAMING IS RETIRED. The April-16 (extended) Israel-Lebanon ceasefire is FRAGILE AND ACTIVELY VIOLATED: Hezbollah — never a formal signatory — has reportedly fired OVER 1,000 DRONES AND OVER 700 ROCKETS at Israel over the past eight days, and IDF Northern Command chief Maj. Gen. Rafi Milo publicly said the command is 'AT WAR' and 'will not tolerate fire on the home front' (campaign CONFIRMED — IDF Northern Command on record; exact 1,000-drone/700-rocket tally REPORTED — single US-official channel relayed by Times of Israel May 25).** A senior US official told Channel 12 + Axios that Washington could GREENLIGHT A LARGER ISRAELI OPERATION against Hezbollah, citing Hezbollah ignoring 'a recent ultimatum'; the IDF has drawn up expanded Lebanon plans (REPORTED — Channel 12 + Axios via TOI). Iran's negotiators treat 'end the war on all fronts including Lebanon' as a precondition of any US-Iran deal (Baghaei restated Monday; an Iranian adviser: 'Without fully restraining America’s rabid dog in Lebanon, there will be no ceasefire'). **Lebanon is now the binding constraint on the Iran deal and the day’s biggest change — the only one of the three structural deal-breakers with a live, escalating kinetic exchange behind it; no expanded ground operation has been launched yet (REPORTED/PREPARATION).** New trigger added at `watching`: US greenlights OR Israel launches an expanded military operation against Hezbollah within 14 days (by 2026-06-08). The existing 'second IDF KIA during 45-day extension' trigger stays as-is; note the kinetic environment has materially intensified beyond the calibrated floor/ceiling described in prior days. PRIOR (May 24): **ISRAEL-LEBANON CEASEFIRE DAY 8 OF 45-DAY EXTENSION — IDF AIRSTRIKES NABATIEH (MAY 22) + AL-HANIYYA (MAY 21) PER HAARETZ WIRE IMAGERY. NO SECOND IDF KIA — MAY 14 NEGEV DAGAN REMAINS THE ONLY CEASEFIRE-WINDOW CASUALTY INSIDE LITANI-AREA SCOPE. PENTAGON-LED SECURITY TRACK STARTING MAY 29 OPERATIONALLY INTACT.** Israeli airstrikes targeted Nabatieh (May 22) and al-Haniyya (May 21) per Haaretz wire imagery. Lebanese health ministry casualty tracking continues from prior week's 18-killed / 124-wounded reading. **No second IDF KIA across May 19-22** (consistent with May 14 Negev Dagan KIA being the only ceasefire-window casualty inside the Litani-area scope). **Hezbollah has not publicly claimed retaliatory mortar/rocket fire in May 19-22 sweep** — silence is itself the negative-space verification. **Both sides continue to operate within unwritten kinetic floor (IDF strikes) and ceiling (no escalation to mass-casualty IDF events)**. The ceasefire-extension architecture (Pentagon-led security track starting May 29) remains operationally intact. **The 'second IDF KIA in southern Lebanon during 45-day ceasefire extension' trigger (added May 16) remains UNTRIPPED Day 8 of 45.** PRIOR (May 19): **A8 ISRAEL-LEBANON DAY 4 OF 45-DAY CEASEFIRE EXTENSION — KINETIC EXCHANGE CONTINUES WITHIN FLOOR/CEILING; LEBANESE CASUALTIES 18 DEAD + 124 WOUNDED PAST DAY (Haaretz live blog May 17, ms.now); cumulative ~2,987 dead / ~9,236 wounded since March 2 (additive).** IDF reported NO new injuries through Monday morning IST; **no second IDF KIA confirmed.** Litani-induced-Israeli-KIA trigger remains active_fired (Dagan May 14); May 16 'second IDF KIA in southern Lebanon during 45-day ceasefire extension window' trigger remains UNTRIPPED. Both sides continue to operate within unwritten kinetic floor (strikes) and ceiling (no escalation to mass-casualty IDF events). **Pentagon security track scheduled for May 29 launch remains on calendar** (May 16 graph trigger 'Pentagon security track May 29 produces public framework deliverable within 30 days of launch (by 2026-06-28)' remains operative). Al Jazeera May 16 'Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon continue despite ceasefire extension' = scare-quoted 'ceasefire' framing Qatari skepticism of architecture; PBS NewsHour and ms.now confirm kinetic continuation; no Hezbollah claim of Israeli mass-casualty event. **PRIOR (May 17): LEBANON CEASEFIRE DAY 3 OF 45-DAY EXTENSION — CONTINUED KINETIC EXCHANGE WITHIN UNWRITTEN FLOOR/CEILING.** On Saturday May 16, IDF conducted strikes 'throughout the day on several areas in southern Lebanon' (Al Jazeera May 16 'Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon continue despite ceasefire extension' — scare-quotes positioning architecture as nominal): Tayr Falsayh (Nabatiyeh), Yanouh, Shahabiya, Qaqaiyat al-Sanoubar (Sidon district), Tuffahta, Ghassaniya, Kawthariyat al-Sayyad. Lebanese health ministry: **2,969 cumulative dead and 9,112 wounded since March 2.** On Sunday morning May 17, **Hezbollah fired several rockets and explosive drones at IDF troops in southern Lebanon** (CONFIRMED — IDF statement, Times of Israel May 17 liveblog primary). Rockets and several drones shot down; some drones exploded near Israeli forces; **NO INJURIES — IDF explicitly stated.** One drone triggered sirens in Misgav Am. **The Litani-induced-Israeli-KIA trigger now counts down at Day 4 of 14 inside the 45-day extension window; the 'second IDF KIA during 45-day extension' trigger added May 16 remains UNTRIPPED.** The architecture-collapse scenario has been further deferred — 48 hours of no second IDF KIA despite continued kinetic exchange validates the cabinet's choice to extend. **Both sides operating within an unwritten kinetic floor and ceiling under the formal ceasefire**: Hezbollah striking IDF without killing IDF; IDF striking Hezbollah infrastructure without conducting Beirut-suburb operations. **PRIOR (May 16):** ISRAEL-LEBANON CEASEFIRE EXTENDED 45 DAYS to ~2026-06-29 with PENTAGON-LED SECURITY TRACK to begin May 29** (CONFIRMED — US State Department announcement May 15, Bloomberg 'Israel, Lebanon Extend Ceasefire 45 Days After Washington Talks,' PBS NewsHour, CGTN, Euronews, Times of Israel liveblog, India TV, CNBC, ms.now, usnews, Foreign Policy). State Department spokesperson Tommy Pigott called the talks 'highly productive.' **Lebanon 'welcomed the extension'** (Times of Israel liveblog primary). Hezbollah was not a participant in the talks but expressed conditional acceptance via SG Naim Qassem's prior 'categorically rejects direct talks' line that does not block Karam-Dermer bilateral. Lebanese officials hoped the negotiations would pave the way for 'tackling thorny issues including the withdrawal of Israeli forces from southern Lebanon and the disarmament of Hezbollah.' Foreign Policy framed it as 'Trump's Lebanon Negotiations Are Breaking the Country' — opposite framing. **The Litani-induced Israeli KIA trigger that fired Day 2 of 14 yesterday continues to count down — the ceasefire extension does not retroactively un-fire the trigger** (Day 3 of 14). **Critically: NO SECOND IDF KIA IN SOUTHERN LEBANON SINCE DAGAN MAY 14 per available reporting through May 16 morning** — the 14-day window's worst-case (second KIA collapses the architecture) has not materialised in the first 48h. **The architecture-collapse scenario the brief warned of has been DEFERRED, NOT FORECLOSED.** Pentagon-led security architecture beginning May 29 substantively elevates the process from State-led ambassadorial to Pentagon-led security architecture. Lebanese press carries the Pigott 'highly productive' line; Lebanese centre-left framing emphasizes Israeli withdrawal timeline as the test. The 512 Lebanese killed by Israeli strikes since April 17 ceasefire (carried forward) remains the structural humanitarian backdrop. PRIOR (May 15): **A4 LITANI-INDUCED ISRAELI KIA TRIGGER FIRED DAY 2 OF 14: Staff Sgt. Negev Dagan, 20, Golani Brigade 12th Battalion killed by Hezbollah mortar fire near the Litani May 14** (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel primary, JNS, i24News, Haaretz, Israeli MoD primary). Total IDF war toll 937. Hezbollah launched 24 attacks on IDF positions, soldiers and military vehicles in southern Lebanon over 24-hour window per Al Jazeera; Dagan KIA is one consequence. **A3 ISRAEL-LEBANON WASHINGTON TALKS DAY 2 CLOSED — STATE DEPARTMENT 'PRODUCTIVE AND POSITIVE'** (Haaretz May 15 liveblog primary). Lebanese delegation under Simon Karam pressed for Israeli troop withdrawal timeline + Lebanese-prisoner release; Israeli delegation under Ron Dermer maintained no immediate withdrawal + prioritized Hezbollah disarmament. **CRITICAL DATE CORRECTION: April-23-extended ceasefire expires 'early hours of Sunday' = May 17, NOT May 15 as yesterday's brief recorded.** Reuters/Manila Times reporting that statement will announce ceasefire extension. **Lebanese Health Ministry: 512 Lebanese killed by Israeli strikes since the April 17 ceasefire implementation** (Al Jazeera May 15). Lebanese press: 13 killed in southern Lebanon strikes (Jibsheet, Kfar Dounine, Nabatieh) including child, woman, Lebanese army member. Lebanese framing (Al Jazeera, Madamsr) emphasizes 512 dead and continued Israeli operations beyond Litani; Israeli framing (JPost, JNS, Haaretz) emphasizes Hezbollah disarmament. **The Litani-KIA firing on Day 2 of 14 stress-tests the Washington architecture exactly as the talks attempt to extend the ceasefire** — Lebanese cabinet under Aoun-Salam faces same political-pressure inversion as Israeli cabinet, with talks pre-positioned for extension announcement. PRIOR (May 14): IDF 'weeklong raid' beyond Litani; Litani-KIA trigger added at Day 1 of 14; talks Day 1. NEAR ZAWTAR AL-SHARQIYAH, LED BY THE GOLANI BRIGADE'S RECONNAISSANCE UNIT — meaningful UPGRADE of yesterday's 'first publicly-acknowledged Litani crossing': IDF itself is now framing the operation as sustained week-long campaign, not one-off crossing** (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel liveblog May 13, JNS, Haaretz). Killed 'some 15 Hezbollah operatives' past 24h; 'some 45 Hezbollah targets' total over 24h including command centres, surveillance posts, weapon depots. Hezbollah fired explosive drones at Israel May 13 — drones triggered Iron Dome interceptor over southern Lebanon that IDF later said was false alarm. Lebanese press: 13 killed in southern Lebanon strikes (Jibsheet, Kfar Dounine, Nabatieh) including child, woman, Lebanese army member; 2 Israeli soldiers wounded (CNN). **NO new Israeli soldier KIA in past 24 hours — new 'Litani-induced Israeli KIA within 14 days' trigger added yesterday is at Day 2 of 14 and HAS NOT activated.** **MAJOR STRUCTURAL FACT: STATE DEPARTMENT CONFIRMED PRIMARY: third round of US-facilitated Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington May 14-15, Lebanese delegation led by Simon Karam, Israeli delegation led by Ron Dermer, agenda explicitly including 'complete disarmament of Hezbollah' alongside 'full restoration of Lebanese state authority'**. The April-23-extended 3-week ceasefire expires around May 15, exactly as talks finish — timing choreographed. Ceasefire status verbal (Zamir's 'no ceasefire' carries forward); coalition-vs-establishment-vs-IDF triangle widens. PRIOR (May 13): (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel liveblog primary May 12). Operation targeted Hezbollah military structures in overnight strikes; explosive drones at troops landed in open areas. **IDF CHIEF OF STAFF EYAL ZAMIR: 'there is no cease-fire in Lebanon' — most senior IDF officer publicly declaring ceasefire dead.** Lebanese Health Ministry ≥13 killed in Israeli attacks over prior 24h; cumulative war toll 2,704 killed / 8,311 wounded (Lebanese tally appears to draw from different reference framework than yesterday's 2,869; either way cumulative count rising daily).
